sections.title: From Demo to Budget Line
beats: 2026 reframed: the question is no longer 'can agents do this' but 'who is paying for it in production'
$13.8B enterprise GenAI spend, ~6x YoY, moving from experimentation budgets to permanent line items
set up the thesis: real money, but the money is not where the demos are

sections.title: Follow the Money, Not the Models
beats: AI ~1/3 of global venture funding; the orchestration/application layer is the fastest-growing share
inference cost for fixed quality fell ~280x in 18 months — models commoditize toward infrastructure
investor takeaway: margin migrates up the stack as the layer below races to zero

sections.title: Reliability Is the Whole Game
beats: capability is not the constraint — autonomous multi-step task success is still a minority
so the product that wins is the one that makes agents trustworthy: eval, guardrails, rollback
proof-point: Klarna's assistant did the work of ~700 agents, sub-2-minute resolution

sections.title: Will Agents Eat the SaaS Seat?
beats: incumbents are repricing: Salesforce Agentforce at ~$2 per resolved conversation, per-outcome not per-seat
pressure-test the replace-seats thesis: real in direction, over-stated in timeline — seats are not collapsing yet
honest qualifier kept in deliberately: the pricing model is changing faster than the seat count

sections.title: The Investable Layer
beats: the moat is orchestration / evaluation / integration + proprietary workflow data, not the weights
what to underwrite (reliability & integration platforms) vs. avoid (thin wrappers, another base model)
tie back to the investor CTA

conclusion.summary: agents crossed into production budgets in 2026; the durable value sits in the reliability/orchestration layer while foundation models commoditize into infrastructure
conclusion.call_to_action: underwrite the orchestration-and-reliability layer; ask founders for agent task-completion and rollback metrics, not demo videos

changes_from_draft: s4 reframed from a flat 'agents replace SaaS' claim to a pressure-test, because research C7 QUALIFIED the thesis — kept honest for a skeptical investor
merged the two token-economics points into s2 so 'follow the money' carries both the funding-share and the cost-collapse argument in one arc
every research_needed marker from the draft outline resolved to a citation id; no open gaps remain
source: content-research-writer step 2 — Iterate on Outline (research folded in, flow fixed, killed claim reframed, section order locked)
